methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - news.vcu.edu
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA signals deliberate community-level capability building in Richmond, attempting to create distributed AI fluency through education and local innovation rather than relying solely on external platform adoption or corporate infrastructure.

signals.title: EPA grant backs community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia - Virginia Tech News
signals.signal_reading: EPA-funded community-driven workforce training in Southwest Virginia represents grassroots capacity building in historically underserved regions, directly countering the concentration pattern by investing in deep local skills development rather than shallow platform adoption.

signals.title: AI trainer says workers must lead technology, not follow it - wdbj7.com
signals.signal_reading: An AI trainer emphasizing worker agency over passive technology consumption in Roanoke signals potential for deep capability development outside NoVA, though limited to those who can access specialized training rather than distributed by default.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: AI pressure on Richmond's young workforce without corresponding deep capability development signals shallow displacement rather than empowerment, potentially widening the gap between those with technical fluency and those merely experiencing AI's labor market effects.

signals.title: Virginia’s AI Data Center Gravy Train Shows Signs of Slowing - Bloomberg Law News
signals.signal_reading: Data center growth slowing in NoVA amid tax debates reveals infrastructure-driven AI concentration may be plateauing, but infrastructure itself doesn't create distributed capability—the capital and technical expertise remain concentrated even if physical expansion slows.
